zh.lifeSummary: 當Alis Bartholomew於1575年2月28日出生在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里 聖 馬丁時，她的父親Thomas Bartelmew45歲，母親Alys Cordray33歲。她於1598年2月12日與Sir Henry John Heath在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1629年2月12日在她的家鄉享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alis Bartholomew于1575年2月28日出生在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里 聖 馬丁时，她的父亲Thomas Bartelmew45岁，母亲Alys Cordray33岁。她于1598年2月12日与Sir Henry John Heath在英国維爾爾索爾茲伯里结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1629年2月12日在她的家乡享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bartholomew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: McFarlane
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Pharthaláin, a patronymic from the personal name Parthalán, which is most probably from Latin Bartholomaeus (see Bartholomew ). Parthalán, regularly shortened in speech to Parlán, has also been Anglicized as Bartlett . Compare McPartland .
History: McFarlane is a Scottish clan originally associated with Arrochar on Loch Long. — Robert McFarlane came to NY in 1835 and was editor of Scientific American magazine (1848–65).

variants.name: Bara
meanings: Polish: from a shortened vernacular form of the personal name Bartłomiej (see Bartholomew ).

Czech (Bára) and Slovak: from a pet form of the female personal name Barbora (Czech), Barbara (Slovak), or possibly of the male personal name Bartoloměj (Czech), Bartolomej (Slovak); see Bartholomew .
Hungarian: from a short form of the personal name Barabás (see Barabas ).

variants.name: Barak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Pinhas, Yoram, Avital, Doron, Dov, Erez, Haim, Hanoch, Moshe, Ronit, Shai, Shimon.

Jewish (Israeli): from the Biblical Hebrew personal name Barak, meaning ‘lightning’. The surname mainly represents a Hebraicized form of several original eastern Ashkenazic surnames having similar consonants.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): acronymic surname from the first letters of the Hebrew patronymic phrase Ben Rabi Kalonymos ‘son of rabbi Kalonymos’. The personal name Kalonymos means ‘beautiful name’ in Greek.

variants.name: Bac
meanings: Polish (also Bać): perhaps a variant of Bacz, from a pet form of the personal name Bartłomiej (see Bartholomew ), occuring in southeastern Poland. Aternatively, it may be a metonymic occupational name derived from baca ‘shepherd’. Compare Batz .
French: from bac ‘tub, trough’, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name or as a topographic name.
Slovenian: nickname from bac ‘lambkin’. Compare Batz .

variants.name: Baert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime.
Flemish and Dutch:
from a short form of the personal name Bartholomaeus (see Bartholomew ), or from the female personal name Baerte or Bartha.

variants.name: Baczek
meanings: Polish:
derivative of the personal name Bacz, Bacza, a pet form of Bartłomiej (see Bartholomew ).
(Bączek): from bączek, a diminutive of bąk, which variously means ‘horse-fly’, ‘bittern’, or ‘eagle-owl’ (see Bak ).

variants.name: Arko
meanings: Slovenian: from a pet form of the personal names beginning with Ar-, most likely from Arnej, an old vernacular form of Bartolomej (see Bartholomew ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from a pet form of the personal name Aron, Slavic form of the Biblical name Aaron .
West African (Ghana): from an Akan male personal name denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Barac
meanings: Croatian: from an old pet form of the personal name Bartolomej (see Bartholomew ) or of its vernacular variants Bartol or Bartul.
Croatian (Barać and Barač): from a derivative of the personal name Bare or Baro, short forms of Bartolomej (see Bartholomew ) or of its vernacular variants Bartol or Bartul.

Serbian (mainly Barać): topographic name derived from bara ‘swamp’ or a habitational name for someone from a place named Bare. It may also be of the same origin as the Croatian names above. Compare Barach .

variants.name: Bach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Johann, Alfons, Bernhard, Erna, Ernst.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, Middle High German bach ‘stream’. This surname is also found in some other European countries, most notably in Denmark and France (Alsace and Lorraine), but also in e.g. the Netherlands, Czechia (see also 5 below), and Croatia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Bach ‘stream, creek’.

variants.name: Baczewski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zygmunt, Bogdan, Bronislaw, Danuta, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund, Zygfryd.
Polish:
habitational name for someone from a place called Bacze in Masovian Voivodeship.

nameMeanings.name: Alis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

variants.name: Alice
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Alis Bartholomew was born on 28 February 1575, in Salisbury St Martin, Wiltshire, England, her father, Thomas Bartelmew, was 45 and her mother, Alys Cordray, was 33. She married Sir Henry John Heath on 12 February 1598, in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 12 February 1629, in her hometown, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alis Bartholomew est née le 28 février 1575, à Salisbury St Martin, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Bartelmew, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Alys Cordray, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Henry John Heath le 12 février 1598, à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 12 février 1629, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Alis Bartholomewが1575年2月28日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリー スト マルティンで生まれた時，父Thomas Bartelmewは45歳，母Alys Cordrayは33歳であった。1598年2月12日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーでSir Henry John Heathと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1629年2月12日に故郷で53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alis Bartholomew(이)가 1575년 2월 28일 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이 스트 마틴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Bartelmew(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alys Cordray(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1598년 2월 12일 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 Sir Henry John Heath(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1629년 2월 12일 그녀의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alis Bartholomew родилась 28 февраля 1575 г. в местности салисбури ст мартинь, уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Bartelmew было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Alys Cordray, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Henry John Heath 12 февраля 1598 г. в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 12 февраля 1629 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 53 лет.
